title: Global Economy - South China Morning Post

items.translated_title: EU carbon tariff sows havoc in China as steel firms grapple with ‘absurd’ rules
items.content:
As trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels continue to rise, China’s firms in the European Union have been forced to walk a delicate tightrope: expanding their presence in the lucrative market while grappling with heightened regulatory hurdles and rapid geopolitical shifts. In the first part of this three-part series, we look at a new, complex EU carbon tariff system that has business owners scratching their heads.
Neil Miao has been exporting metal hardware to Europe for years. But earlier...

items.title: Japan’s property sector looks strong. So why are investors going abroad?
items.creator: Nicholas Spiro
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Japan’s property sector looks strong. So why are investors going abroad?
items.content:
When it comes to the performance of real estate markets in the Asia-Pacific, Japan reigns supreme. Asia’s second largest economy is the deepest, most widely traded, and the safest market in the region. Last year, Japan accounted for 28 per cent of direct investment in Asia-Pacific commercial real estate, data from MSCI shows.
The average vacancy rate for grade A offices in Tokyo was 0.7 per cent in the first quarter of this year. Rents have risen for nine straight quarters, increasing 13.2 per...

items.title: After US-Iran accord and oil-price plunge, analysts warn of ‘grey area’ for Chinese firms
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Kandy Wong
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: After US-Iran accord and oil-price plunge, analysts warn of ‘grey area’ for Chinese firms
items.content:
As plunging oil prices signal broad market optimism following an initial deal between the United States and Iran, attention has turned to whether Chinese firms sanctioned for Iran-related business will see immediate relief, though analysts remain cautious.
“New purchases of Iranian oil ostensibly wouldn’t be subject to fresh sanctions, but at the same time, I imagine we won’t see a rush to lift existing sanctions on Chinese importers of Iranian oil,” said Lynn Song, chief economist for Greater...

items.title: Will a US-Iran deal ease pressure on China’s sanctions-hit firms?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Will a US-Iran deal ease pressure on China’s sanctions-hit firms?

images.alt: Tugboats move a crude oil tanker to its berth in Qingdao, in China’s eastern Shandong province. The US has sanctioned several Chinese independent, or “teapot”, refineries that purchase Iranian crude. Photo: AFP
images.title: Tugboats move a crude oil tanker to its berth in Qingdao, in China’s eastern Shandong province. The US has sanctioned several Chinese independent, or “teapot”, refineries that purchase Iranian crude. Photo: AFP

items.content:
A tentative US-Iran deal has eased immediate fears of escalation, but the fine print around sanctions relief – following months of aggressive US enforcement under the “Economic Fury” campaign – remains highly contested and legally intricate.
The South China Morning Post unpacks the key sanctions-related terms in the agreement, the intense US sanctions campaign against Chinese entities since the outbreak of the Iran conflict in late February, and what a potential waiver or termination of those...

items.title: China’s first C919s get critical ‘C-checks’ as European regulators watch
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s first C919s get critical ‘C-checks’ as European regulators watch

images.alt: China’s C919 has entered an important phase, with jets across the series undergoing rigorous inspections. Photo: Handout
images.title: China’s C919 has entered an important phase, with jets across the series undergoing rigorous inspections. Photo: Handout

items.content:
Three years after its maiden commercial flight as China’s flagship civilian airliner, the C919 is entering a pivotal maintenance phase, with jets across the series being progressively grounded for rigorous inspections.
The results of these safety-focused “C-checks” are expected to play a role in convincing European aviation regulators of the aircraft’s long-term airworthiness.
State-owned carrier China Southern Airlines finished the first C-check of its C919 fleet at the end of May. The process...

items.title: Strait of Hormuz still eerily quiet as doubts remain over ‘fragile’ peace deal
items.creator: Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Strait of Hormuz still eerily quiet as doubts remain over ‘fragile’ peace deal
items.content:
The Strait of Hormuz was still largely devoid of shipping traffic in the hours following the announcement of a US-Iran peace deal, as analysts warned it remained uncertain how and when normal operations along the waterway would be restored.
The only vessel moving in the strait on Monday morning was broadcasting its status as a patrol ship, according to data from vessel tracking platform MarineTraffic, as hundreds of commercial ships remained stranded in the region.
A liquefied natural gas tanker...

items.title: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food safety issues’ (原标题: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food-safety issues’)

items.translated_title: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food safety issues’
items.content:
Chinese authorities have summoned representatives of US-based membership-only retailer Sam’s Club for an “accountability interview” in response to “frequent food-safety issues”, the State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) announced on Monday.
According to a notice from the regulator, the Walmart-owned supermarket chain was urged to “prioritise food safety, strictly fulfil social corporate responsibilities, mitigate risks across the entire supply chain and safeguard public health” in...

items.title: Abu Dhabi to tap Chinese tech to scale up green economy, energy chief says
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Abu Dhabi to tap Chinese tech to scale up green economy, energy chief says
items.content:
The Abu Dhabi government is fast-tracking its push to extensively tap into China’s technologies, from renewable energy and electric vehicles (EVs) to robotics, as it pursues a greener economy, according to its energy chief.
Abdulla Humaid Al Jarwan, chairman of the Abu Dhabi department of energy, told the SCMP that his department was in talks with 22 Chinese firms, including Contemporary Amperex Technology Ltd (CATL), during a recent visit to Shanghai, and expected a deepened tie-up with China’s...

items.title: AIIB head Zou Jiayi on why multilateralism is an ‘infrastructure of global trust’
items.creator: Ji Siqi
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: AIIB head Zou Jiayi on why multilateralism is an ‘infrastructure of global trust’
items.content:
Zou Jiayi is president and chairwoman of the board of directors of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB), a multilateral development lender headquartered in Beijing and inaugurated in 2016.
Before leading AIIB, Zou was deputy secretary general of the national committee of the Chinese People’s Political Consultative Conference, China’s top political advisory body. She previously held senior roles at the Ministry of Finance, including as vice-minister, and as part of her three decades of...

items.title: At World Cup, Mexico leans on China tech and transport to keep the tournament kicking
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: At World Cup, Mexico leans on China tech and transport to keep the tournament kicking
items.content:
Although China has no team on the pitch at the 2026 World Cup, the country’s industrial and technological presence is dominating the tournament’s logistics in Mexico.
China has bolstered Mexico’s capacity as a host country with railways and buses, as well as the tournament’s official footballs. And that support comes at a time when the Latin American country is navigating its relations with Beijing amid mounting pressure from Washington.
Among the bigger-ticket items on a long list of support...

items.title: Why China is attracting wealthy Gulf families in search of education, tech and business
items.creator: He Huifeng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why China is attracting wealthy Gulf families in search of education, tech and business
items.content:
In April, a family from the United Arab Emirates – four adults, three children and a nanny – spent about 300,000 yuan (US$44,228) on a seven-day educational and business tour to China.
The trip was nearly cancelled as the Iran war repeatedly disrupted their flights and travel plans, but they eventually arrived in Shanghai.
“Their willingness to make the trip was unwavering,” said Lilian Liu at Middle East-focused tour operator UFOX Travel, who hosted the family. Such clients barely existed a few...

items.title: China’s Geely Auto to slash excess capacity amid overhaul to boost carmaker’s global edge
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s Geely Auto to slash excess capacity amid overhaul to boost carmaker’s global edge
items.content:
Geely Auto, which is locked in a fierce battle for dominance against BYD in China’s crowded automotive market, has pledged to purge excess capacity through an asset restructuring while ramping up its go-global drive with an eye on greater international competitiveness.
Chairman Li Shufu said during the Chongqing Auto Show on Friday that the Hong Kong-listed carmaker would assess the oversupply of capacity across all units to determine whether to close, suspend, merge or sell redundant production...

items.title: Where will investors go if or when the AI bubble pops?
items.creator: Anthony Rowley
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Where will investors go if or when the AI bubble pops?
items.content:
When does euphoria turn to panic? The question is highly relevant to stock market psychology now as investor and entrepreneur obsession with artificial intelligence (AI) morphs into apparent anxiety that the boom is about to go bust and as stock prices wobble in consequence.
A principal sign that AI mania is turning into malaise and malady is that tech barons are seeking frantically to raise outrageous sums of capital while they perceive the going to still be good. Another is the growing...

items.title: Do China’s export curbs on tungsten threaten Japan’s AI chip supply chain?
items.creator: Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Do China’s export curbs on tungsten threaten Japan’s AI chip supply chain?
items.content:
Two major Japanese chemical manufacturers could halt production of a gas crucial to AI chipmaking starting next month, as supply tightens and prices surge amid Beijing’s export controls on tungsten.
The price of tungsten hexafluoride has jumped more than 200 per cent year on year because of supply bottlenecks, coupled with rising chip demand, market data showed. The gas is a critical precursor in the semiconductor industry, where it is used to develop the microscopic connections inside advanced...

items.translated_title: US-China talks need to be ‘institutionalised’ to ease tensions in AI era: Haass
items.content:
The United States and China should hold regular high-level meetings and deepen cooperation on emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, as greater transparency is essential to avoid a downturn in relations, a veteran US diplomat has said.
Richard Haass, president emeritus of the Council on Foreign Relations, said meetings between senior officials from Washington and Beijing should become routine, rather than being treated as “news”.
“This is a relationship that needs to be...

items.title: 95% of overseas firms expect to sustain or boost use of the yuan: Bank of China poll
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: 95% of overseas firms expect to sustain or boost use of the yuan: Bank of China poll
items.content:
The global business appetite for China’s yuan is strengthening as Beijing’s long-term campaign to internationalise its currency gains traction, according to a new industry white paper.
More than 95 per cent of overseas companies expect to maintain or increase their cross-border yuan settlements over the coming year, according to the latest findings from Bank of China, one of the country’s leading state-owned lenders.
The report, released this week, showed that 34.5 per cent of surveyed overseas...

items.title: EVs capture two-thirds of China’s car market in record-breaking week
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: EVs capture two-thirds of China’s car market in record-breaking week
items.content:
Electric vehicles accounted for a record 66.7 per cent of new car sales in mainland China during the first week of June, the latest sign that the country’s battery-powered carmakers are benefiting from the global energy crisis.
Two out of every three new cars sold on the mainland in the seven days ending June 7 were either pure electric or plug-in hybrid vehicles, according to data from the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA).
The penetration rate climbed from 62.9 per cent in May as a wave...

items.title: China’s ports are by far the most efficient in the world: World Bank study
items.creator: Carol Yang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s ports are by far the most efficient in the world: World Bank study
items.content:
China’s ports continued to dominate global efficiency rankings in 2025, with seven Chinese trade hubs placing in the top 10, according to a study by the World Bank and S&P Global released on Wednesday.
The latest edition of the annual report comes at a time when ports are playing a more vital role in the global economy than ever, as facilities strive to handle intense disruptions to global supply chains amid the aftermath of the Red Sea crisis and the ongoing fallout from the US-Israel war on...

items.title: Why tech titans’ IPOs matter as much in China as in the US
items.creator: James David Spellman
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why tech titans’ IPOs matter as much in China as in the US
items.content:
Blockbuster initial public offerings from tech champions such as Anthropic and SpaceX are about to fundamentally reshape global capital markets.
This wave will redirect capital flows and alter valuations across the tech sector. As competition intensifies, investors worldwide will be forced to completely rethink where future returns will come from. For China, the rise of these companies will intensify an already fierce contest for global capital, putting further strain on foreign investment...

items.translated_title: China’s central bank head says country a haven for asset diversification in volatile world
items.content:
China’s central bank has described the country’s markets as a haven for diversified allocation amid geopolitical tension and market volatility, as the authorities proceed with an ambitious plan to increase foreign participation in domestic capital markets and promote the overseas use of the yuan.
“China’s financial market depth and breadth provide important opportunities for overseas institutions seeking diversified allocations in a period of heightened global financial volatility,” People’s...
